extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is an inductive invariant preserved by every transition. Verification: (1) sent becomes true ONLY via t_send_allowed, which is guarded by `not opted_out`, so post-state has opted_out=false (antecedent false, implication holds). (2) opted_out becomes true ONLY via t_optout, which simultaneously sets sent:=false (so opted_out=true with sent=false, implication holds). (3) No transition ever sets opted_out back to false, so once opted_out is true it stays true; while it is true, t_send_allowed is permanently disabled (guard not opted_out fails), so sent can never be re-raised to true — send_attempt fires t_send_blocked (sent:=false), tick sets sent:=false, and a repeated optout sets sent:=false. Inductive check across all four transitions from any state satisfying the invariant: t_optout -> (opted_out=true, sent=false) OK; t_send_allowed -> opted_out remains false OK; t_send_blocked -> sets sent=false OK; t_tick -> sets sent=false OK. Initial state opted_out=false trivially satisfies it. Therefore no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true; the property holds over the entire input space (well within the 16-event bound).
